Validated across pico-scale, nano, and micro runs: mixing Question/Answer pairs into pretraining works; the separate finetune stage collapsed tiny models into repetition. The collapse was root-caused to an off-by-one bug in the finetune target construction (a copy objective), now fixed. Post-fix measurement: no more collapse, but plain finetuning scored slightly below the base model on every axis, so the finding stands in a weaker form.

The pretrain stage renders instruction pairs as light Question/Answer text and mixes them into the corpus at instruction_fraction of tokens, teaching instruction-following jointly with fluency. The finetune stage continues training on the same pairs and format afterward. Empirically, the base pretrained model stays fluent and referent-free, but the finetuned checkpoint degenerates into repetitive, low-grammar output in three independent runs, despite eliminating the earlier suspected causes (role control tokens, format mismatch).

Confirmed instances: (1) an early 60M-parameter run at a bad tokens-per-parameter ratio, (2) the nano scale-ladder run (report_pretrain grammar 8.18 versus report_sft grammar 1.7, with literal repeated-character output), (3) the micro run showing coherent, referent-free behavior in the interactive pretrain-model session, consistent with the same collapse pattern though no separate sft report was surfaced for it. The mechanism was traced to a concrete bug rather than only training dynamics: render_pair_example in data.py built labels aligned to the same index as the input tokens instead of the next token, while the model computes cross entropy of the logits at position i against target i with no internal shift (the convention the packed pretraining data follows). The separate finetune stage was therefore the only path training a copy objective (predict the current answer token from itself), which produces exactly the observed repeated-token collapse, and explains why co-trained instruction following (which goes through the correctly shifted packed path) worked while the separate stage did not. The bug is fixed. Catastrophic forgetting from a second phase with no pretraining replay remains a plausible secondary factor for a tiny model, but is no longer the leading explanation. Decision (superseding the earlier recommendation to make finetune opt-in): keep the finetune stage as a default and improve it gradually, since SFT experiments are cheap relative to data generation and the scale-world runner (node 43) now trains and evaluates finetune at every rung on each run, producing a report_sft at pico, nano, micro, and full. That gives repeated, low-cost signal to iterate on the finetuning approach (for example replaying pretraining data during finetuning to prevent the forgetting that drives the collapse, or lighter-touch adaptation) rather than removing the stage. Post-fix measurement on the broadened corpus (full rung, updated evaluation): the collapse is gone, the finetuned model produces fluent, instruction-shaped output, but plain finetuning scored slightly below the base pretrained model on every axis (grammar 4.44 versus 5.62, coherence 3.72 versus 4.84, accuracy about 1.4 for both), consistent with mild residual forgetting. The dominant remaining failure, semantic drift and unstable referent binding, is shared by the base model and is a pretraining-scale property, not an SFT problem. The consolidated response (node 15): a single default finetune configuration with pretraining-data replay and validation early stopping, with the sweep harness retained for comparisons.
